patch.value: ADRs are recorded as closed Decision items parented here. They document decisions ALREADY made (recorded today), so they are closed records, not open work. New decisions append to this log over time.
Living log of Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) for pm-cli. Each ADR is a recorded Decision item (context/decision/consequences/evidence) capturing a real architectural choice already made.
Make the 'why' behind the architecture discoverable as durable context
Agents and contributors understand prior decisions without re-deriving them from code/memory
Every architecturally significant decision has an ADR child recorded in the PR that implements it; ADR log discoverable via pm list scoped to this epic.

## Charter
Living log of Architecture Decision Records. Each ADR is a Decision item under this epic capturing context, the decision, consequences, and evidence for an architectural choice actually made (not aspirational). 38 closed ADRs at 2026-07-04 (e.g. pm-w13j literal all-src coverage gate).

## Conventions
- ADR titles: "ADR: <date or topic> — <decision>".
- ADRs close when the decision is recorded and its consequences are reflected in code/docs; they are historical records, so closed ADRs are never edited retroactively (append comments instead).
- One open child: pm-96wm (2026-06-07 deep-audit ADR) stays open as its remediation batches are still being verified; close it once every deferred finding it indexes is filed or shipped.

## Charter
Living log of Architecture Decision Records. Each ADR is a Decision item under this epic capturing context, the decision, consequences, and evidence for an architectural choice actually made (not aspirational). 38 closed ADRs at 2026-07-04 (e.g. pm-w13j literal all-src coverage gate).

## Conventions
- ADR titles: "ADR: <date or topic> — <decision>".
- ADRs close when the decision is recorded and its consequences are reflected in code/docs; they are historical records, so closed ADRs are never edited retroactively (append comments instead).
- One open child: pm-96wm (2026-06-07 deep-audit ADR) stays open as its remediation batches are still being verified; close it once every deferred finding it indexes is filed or shipped.

## Update 2026-07-05
Two new proposed ADRs added to the log: pm-pbyu (noun-verb CLI grammar + alias-preserving deprecation policy, for epic pm-n7rr) and pm-muhw (SDK is the single public API; CLI/MCP are presentation layers, for epic pm-usfg). Open = proposed; close = accepted.

author: claude-code-agent

patch.value:
## Charter
Living log of Architecture Decision Records. Each ADR is a Decision item under this epic capturing context, the decision, consequences, and evidence for an architectural choice. The log includes both proposed ADRs (open) and accepted historical records (closed).

## Conventions
- ADR titles: "ADR: <date or topic> — <decision>".
- Open ADRs are proposals under active review; they may be edited before acceptance.
- Closing an ADR means the decision text is accepted as architecture context, not that every implementation child has shipped. Implementation remains tracked by the linked epics/features/tasks.
- Accepted/closed ADRs are historical records: do not rewrite their decision text retroactively; append comments or create a superseding ADR instead.
- Current open children: pm-96wm (2026-06-07 deep-audit ADR remediation verification), pm-pbyu (noun-verb CLI grammar), and pm-muhw (SDK public API layering).

## Update 2026-07-05
Two new proposed ADRs added to the log: pm-pbyu (noun-verb CLI grammar + alias-preserving deprecation policy, for epic pm-n7rr) and pm-muhw (SDK is the public programmatic API; CLI/MCP become presentation layers as primitives are promoted, for epic pm-usfg). Open = proposed; close = accepted.

message: Clarify ADR proposed vs accepted lifecycle conventions

patch.value:
## Charter
Living log of Architecture Decision Records. Each ADR is a Decision item under this epic capturing context, the decision, consequences, and evidence for an architectural choice. The log includes both proposed ADRs (open) and accepted historical records (closed).

## Conventions
- ADR titles: "ADR: <date or topic> — <decision>".
- Open ADRs are proposals under active review; they may be edited before acceptance.
- Closing an ADR means the decision text is accepted as architecture context, not that every implementation child has shipped. Implementation remains tracked by the linked epics/features/tasks.
- Accepted/closed ADRs are historical records: do not rewrite their decision text retroactively; append comments or create a superseding ADR instead.
- Current open children: pm-96wm (2026-06-07 deep-audit ADR remediation verification), pm-pbyu (noun-verb CLI grammar), and pm-muhw (SDK public API layering).

## Update 2026-07-05
Two new proposed ADRs added to the log: pm-pbyu (noun-verb CLI grammar + alias-preserving deprecation policy, for epic pm-n7rr) and pm-muhw (SDK is the public programmatic API; CLI/MCP become presentation layers as primitives are promoted, for epic pm-usfg). Open = proposed; close = accepted.

## Update 2026-07-06
New proposed ADR: **pm-bl8x — workspace scale-out strategy** (read index format, directory fan-out, bounded-output/pagination contract, claim scaling, git growth policy). Governs implementation children of initiative epic pm-9rxu; open=proposed, close=accepted.
